evals.prompt: Turn this vague feature request into an implementation-ready issue: add a way for users to save articles for later from both the article page and search results.
evals.expected_output: A frontend feature issue that explicitly classifies the request, follows frontend + feature guidance, and defines concrete behavior for both entry points.
assertions: Explicitly labels the request as Surface: Frontend and Work type: Feature, or uses equivalent unambiguous classification.
Follows the frontend fork by naming visible states, interactions, accessibility expectations, or responsive constraints relevant to save/unsave behavior.
Follows the feature fork by describing the new behavior, affected users, and any rollout or compatibility constraints if relevant.
Makes the two entry points (article page and search results) observable in scope and acceptance criteria.
Does not misroute the request as backend-only, bugfix, refactor, improvement, or research-backed change.

evals.prompt: Write a bugfix issue for intermittent 500 errors on POST /orders when a guest user retries checkout after a payment timeout.
evals.expected_output: A backend bugfix issue that explicitly classifies the request, follows backend + bugfix guidance, and captures repro-sensitive failure handling.
assertions: Explicitly labels the request as Surface: Backend and Work type: Bugfix, or uses equivalent unambiguous classification.
Follows the backend fork by naming rules, contracts, persistence impact, or failure handling for POST /orders.
Follows the bugfix fork by stating current behavior, expected behavior, repro conditions, impact, and regression-sensitive paths.
Separates confirmed scope from assumptions or open questions if the root cause is unknown.
Does not misroute the request as frontend/fullstack, feature, refactor, improvement, or research-backed change.

evals.prompt: Define a refactor issue for splitting the billing service into clearer modules while preserving current invoice and refund behavior.
evals.expected_output: A backend refactor issue that explicitly classifies the request, follows backend + refactor guidance, and preserves existing behavior boundaries.
assertions: Explicitly labels the request as Surface: Backend and Work type: Refactor, or uses equivalent unambiguous classification.
Follows the backend fork by naming contracts, data or persistence impact, and failure or safety handling where relevant.
Follows the refactor fork by naming preserved behavior, the design problem, and clear safety boundaries.
Defines non-goals that prevent the issue from drifting into feature work.
Does not misroute the request as feature, bugfix, improvement, or research-backed change.

evals.prompt: Turn this fullstack request into a ready issue: let workspace admins invite a teammate by email, show pending invites in the UI, and reject duplicate active invites.
evals.expected_output: A fullstack feature issue that explicitly classifies the request, follows fullstack + feature guidance, and covers both the UI and backend contract.
assertions: Explicitly labels the request as Surface: Fullstack and Work type: Feature, or uses equivalent unambiguous classification.
Follows the fullstack fork by naming the end-to-end flow, the frontend/backend seam contract, and any sequencing or ownership split.
Follows the feature fork by describing the new behavior for admins and the affected workflow.
Includes acceptance criteria for invite creation, pending invite visibility, and duplicate invite rejection across both surfaces.
Does not collapse the issue into frontend-only or backend-only guidance.

evals.prompt: Turn this vague request into an implementation-ready issue: users say the settings page is confusing and should be easier to use.
evals.expected_output: A frontend improvement issue that explicitly classifies the request, follows frontend + improvement guidance, and converts vague UX feedback into observable behavior changes without inventing net-new product scope.
assertions: Explicitly labels the request as Surface: Frontend and Work type: Improvement, or uses equivalent unambiguous classification.
Follows the frontend fork by naming current states, interaction pain points, accessibility considerations, or responsive constraints for the settings page.
Follows the improvement fork by stating what becomes better, how the improvement will be observed, and what stays unchanged.
Turns vague feedback into observable scope and acceptance criteria instead of generic wording like 'make it easier to use'.
Does not misroute the request as a net-new feature, backend change, refactor, or research-backed change without evidence from the prompt.

evals.prompt: Define an implementation-ready issue for this research-backed change: after reviewing 40 onboarding support calls, we found users miss the verification email because the post-signup screen gives no next-step guidance. Update the experience so more new users complete verification without changing the email delivery system.
evals.expected_output: An issue that routes to research-backed change and uses the evidence source to shape scope, intended effect, and boundaries. Because the prompt excludes delivery-system changes, it should stay on the product-surface guidance established by the request.
assertions: Explicitly labels the request as Surface: Frontend and Work type: Research-backed change, or uses equivalent unambiguous classification.
Follows the research-backed fork by naming the evidence source, key finding, rationale, and intended effect on verification completion.
Follows the frontend fork by naming visible post-signup states, guidance, interactions, accessibility expectations, or responsive constraints.
Keeps the email delivery system out of scope because the prompt explicitly excludes it.
Does not misroute the request as a backend system fix, generic feature, or vague improvement without referencing the supplied research evidence.

evals.prompt: Write an implementation-ready issue to reduce report generation time for the standard monthly export from about 12 seconds to under 5 seconds without changing report contents or the export UI.
evals.expected_output: A backend improvement issue that explicitly classifies the request, uses an observable performance target, and preserves existing behavior and UI boundaries.
assertions: Explicitly labels the request as Surface: Backend and Work type: Improvement, or uses equivalent unambiguous classification.
Follows the improvement fork by naming what gets better, how it will be observed, and what stays unchanged.
Follows the backend fork by naming affected processing rules, data flow, jobs, persistence, or failure handling relevant to report generation.
Preserves report contents and the export UI as non-goals or unchanged behavior boundaries.
Does not misroute the request as a frontend change, refactor-only task, or net-new feature.
